title: Pinalia vagans
taxonomy: Pinalia vagans is classified within the genus Pinalia and family Orchidaceae. The genus Pinalia is a group of orchids that are native to tropical Asia. Orchidaceae is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants, comprising over 30,000 species. Pinalia vagans, like other members of the genus, is a type of terrestrial orchid. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and evolutionary history.

intro: Pinalia vagans, also known as the "Himalayan orchid," is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to the Himalayas and surrounding regions. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as fever, cough, and digestive problems.
